recentReviews.author: Rainer L.
recentReviews.text: The app is completely unusable. On two Android devices, after entering a username and password, it prompts for video identification and then aborts the process with the message that it cannot continue the identification "due to the information provided." However, I didn't provide any information and wasn't asked to. Access works on my computer. However, there's no support information anywhere. Utter garbage.

recentReviews.author: Michael L
recentReviews.text: better interest rate for newbies, nothing for existinf customers

recentReviews.author: Julijus Stefan
recentReviews.text: The latest update is a huge UX downgrade, especially for CFD. I can no longer see my avg position price without having to tap on each position. And I can no longer see P/L per instrument from a dashboard, only total daily P/L. Whoever designed this update probably hasn't been trading in his life and has no clue what the user experience should look like. I now have to download an old APK file from the internet just to avoid using this awful looking CFD dashboard on the latest update

recentReviews.text: good trading app, my one issue is it's not clear how dividends are reinvested. Got ?17 sitting in a pie with everything sitting at 2%. , it won't reinvest because the buy targets of the pie are met? ??

recentReviews.author: Slawomir Gromadzki
recentReviews.text: I am extremely disappointed with Trading 212. I recently transferred ?4,014 into my account, only to find that ?14 was immediately deducted as a "deposit fee." Because I had an old account from years ago, I was hit with a 0.7% fee that was not made clearly visible during the transaction. I realized the mistake and withdrew the money immediately, but Trading 212 has kept the ?14 for a service I didn't even use. It feels predatory to charge "lifetime" fees on old accounts without a clear, upfron

recentReviews.author: Sam Hobday
recentReviews.text: Some pros and cons. Ideally suited for complete amateurs and casual stock trading. CFD trading is set up so the house wins. Completely unprofessional system where margins and leverage for different trades are all tied and "stacked together". Etoro is much more professional and clearly the percentage of people NOT losing money there compared to 212 speaks volumes. the fact they take 3 days to transfer your money is a complete joke too.

recentReviews.author: Masny CZ
recentReviews.text: When I started using this application, its simplicity and clarity were an advantage. But in the roughly 14 months of use, the user interface (including functionality) has changed so many times that it actually detracts from the clarity and user experience. The latest update with the drop-down list is a good example of this.

recentReviews.author: Sporty
recentReviews.text: 1.) Long-term investing: ++Simple and logical operation. ++Use a daily (!) savings plan to buy my favorites at an average price. Only a few providers offer this so far (IBKR, Robinhood) --Moderate spreads but unfortunately additional USD exchange rate costs. 2.) Shorter-term trading: --I'm not currently using this here. Please also offer the option of account management in USD for CFDs, so that currency fees aren't charged on every trade of US stocks.
